content.full_text:
913420-

GIFTS, PERSONAL USE
Telephone Advice 6/25/91
Section 89513(f)(2), 89513(f), 89505, 89513.

SUM
Meals provided to staffers by Senator as business lunches are unlimited if directly related to a pol
purpose. Factors: Not extravagant; solely for business; necessary; infrequent. Where staffer is
required to be there and work, the lunches were treated as compensation and not subject to the
gift limit in 89505.


FOR FILE
Senator Newton Russell asked whether he could use campaign funds to purchase gifts for
two staffers who were getting married. I informed him that Section 89513(f)(2) of the personal
use provisions specifically permits gifts of $250 per calendar year to staffers for any reason.
Therefore he could provide the gifts to the staffers so long as the aggregate amount of all gifts to
the staffer does not exceed $250 per year.

He then asked whether he had to count business lunches provided to his staffers as gifts to
them under the $250 limit. I explained that the statute contemplated unlimited gifts where the
gifts are directly related to a political purpose. Thus, he could provide $250 in personal gifts
from campaign funds to his staffer and ignore all directly related gifts in tracking the gifts for
purposes of the $250 limit.

I then explained that business lunches could be directly related gifts depending on the
facts. He stated that he took his district staff to lunch because it was a convenient time for them
to go over the developments in the district. He stated the lunches were not extravagant. I
explained that where the lunches were solely for business they would be directly related.
However, if the lunches were intended as a reward to staffers or were only partly business they
would not be directly related gifts and were permissible only under the $250 limit. Other factors
relevant to determining whether the lunches were directly related which were discussed were
necessary and frequency. Where not necessary the provision of the meals looked more like a
reward or gift. Where overly frequent the provision of meals looked like it was not necessary.

Finally, internally we discussed the nature of the free meal with respect to the recipient.
If the staffer was receiving a gift he would be subject to the $250 gift limit (Section 89505,
separate from the personal use limit). Consequently, the aggregate of directly related gifts and
other gifts within the $250 limit of Section 89513(f)(2), could not exceed $250 in any case.
However, the advice meeting group decided that where the business lunch was solely a business
lunch and staffers were required to be there, the lunches would be construed as in the nature of
compensation (payment of business expenses the staffers would incur or fringe benefits to
staffers). Consequently, the recipients would not be receiving gifts but would be receiving
additional income.

sections.question_synthetic: Senator Newton Russell sought clarification on whether he could use campaign funds for staff gifts (including wedding gifts and business lunches) and how such gifts should be counted against gift limits under California's personal use and gift statutes.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Campaign funds may be used to provide up to $250 in personal gifts annually to staffers; business lunches directly related to political purposes and required attendance are unlimited and treated as compensation rather than gifts, and are not subject to gift limits if they are not extravagant, solely business-related, necessary, and infrequent.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is telephone advice from 6/12/1991; no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present; content reconstructed from narrative discussion format; some text appears to contain OCR errors (e.g., 'igrrore', 'exffavagant', 'intemally', 'nafure', 'lactors'); citation format suggests this is internal FPPC memorandum rather than formal response letter

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Senator Newton Russell sought clarification on whether he could use campaign funds for staff gifts (including wedding gifts and business lunches) and how such gifts should be counted against gift limits under California's personal use and gift statutes.

CONCLUSION: Campaign funds may be used to provide up to $250 in personal gifts annually to staffers; business lunches directly related to political purposes and required attendance are unlimited and treated as compensation rather than gifts, and are not subject to gift limits if they are not extravagant, solely business-related, necessary, and infrequent.

SUM Meals provided to staffers by Senator as business lunches are unlimited if directly related to a pol purpose. Factors: Not extravagant; solely for business; necessary; infrequent. Where staffer is required to be there and work, the lunches were treated as compensation and not subject to the gift limit in 89505. FOR FILE Senator Newton Russell asked whether he could use campaign funds to purchase gifts for two staffers who were getting married. I informed him that Section 89513(f)(2) of the personal use provisions specifically permits gifts of $250 per calendar year to staffers for any reason. Finally, internally we discussed the nature of the free meal with respect to the recipient. If the staffer was receiving a gift he would be subject to the $250 gift limit (Section 89505, separate from the personal use limit). embedding.summary: FPPC telephone advice addressing Senator Newton Russell's questions about using campaign funds for staff gifts and meals, concluding that personal gifts are limited to $250 per staffer annually while business lunches directly related to political purposes and required attendance are treated as compensation and are unlimited.
